subject: Information I
notes: This is an original project decomposition for planning and coverage review.
All unit objectives, sequencing, and assessments are project-authored interpretations, not MEXT wording or MEXT-prescribed requirements.
The Course of Study and commentary locators define review scope; lesson counts, titles, examples, and evidence rules remain project decisions.
No official textbook, final curriculum alignment, approval, or publication claim is made.

units.title: Information Society and Problem Solving

source_locator.normative: High School Course of Study, Information I content (1), PDF pages 191-192
source_locator.commentary: Information commentary, Information I content (1), PDF pages 30-34

units.unit_outcome: Analyze an information-related problem, identify responsibilities and constraints, compare alternatives, and evaluate a solution with evidence.

lessons.title: Information and Media

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish data, information, and media in a concrete situation.
learning_objectives.label: A1.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how a medium changes what can be recorded, transmitted, or interpreted.
learning_objectives.label: A1.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Identify an information-related problem from observations and evidence.
learning_objectives.label: A1.O3

prerequisites: Everyday experience receiving and sharing information.
lessons.assessment_intent: Classify examples and justify a problem statement using observable evidence.

lessons.title: Reliability and Source Evaluation

learning_objectives.statement: Separate a claim from the evidence offered for it.
learning_objectives.label: A2.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate source relevance, currency, authorship, and corroboration.
learning_objectives.label: A2.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Record uncertainty instead of presenting an unsupported conclusion.
learning_objectives.label: A2.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Compare candidate sources and explain which evidence can and cannot support a claim.

lessons.title: Rights, Responsibility, Privacy, and Risk

learning_objectives.statement: Identify stakeholders, information flows, and potential harms in a scenario.
learning_objectives.label: A3.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish personal responsibility, social norms, and source-backed legal constraints.
learning_objectives.label: A3.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why information security matters and propose a risk-reducing action with trade-offs.
learning_objectives.label: A3.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Analyze a fictional scenario, explain the importance of information security, and avoid unsupported legal claims.

lessons.title: Problem Definition and Requirements

learning_objectives.statement: Describe the current and desired states of a problem separately.
learning_objectives.label: A4.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Identify stakeholders, constraints, and measurable success criteria.
learning_objectives.label: A4.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Rewrite a solution-shaped request as a testable problem statement.
learning_objectives.label: A4.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Create and critique a problem definition for a school-neutral fictional context.

lessons.title: Decomposition and Modeling

learning_objectives.statement: Decompose a problem into related questions and tasks.
learning_objectives.label: A5.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Represent relevant entities, relationships, inputs, and outputs in a simple model.
learning_objectives.label: A5.O2

learning_objectives.statement: State what a model intentionally leaves out.
learning_objectives.label: A5.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Create a simple model, state its omissions, and compare decompositions against the stated requirements.

lessons.title: Comparing and Evaluating Solutions

learning_objectives.statement: Generate more than one candidate solution to a defined problem.
learning_objectives.label: A6.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Compare solutions using requirements, evidence, risk, and trade-offs.
learning_objectives.label: A6.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Design a small evaluation and use its result to recommend an improvement.
learning_objectives.label: A6.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Use a decision matrix and written justification without treating a score as automatic truth.

lessons.title: Information Society Inquiry Project

learning_objectives.statement: Investigate a bounded information-society problem with traceable evidence.
learning_objectives.label: A7.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Analyze reciprocal effects between information technology and social practice, then propose and evaluate a response.
learning_objectives.label: A7.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Communicate limitations and next questions.
learning_objectives.label: A7.O3

prerequisites: A3: Rights, Responsibility, Privacy, and Risk.
lessons.assessment_intent: Performance task with separate evidence, reasoning, proposal, and reflection criteria.

units.title: Communication and Information Design

source_locator.normative: High School Course of Study, Information I content (2), PDF page 192
source_locator.commentary: Information commentary, Information I content (2), PDF pages 34-38

units.unit_outcome: Design, test, and improve an accessible information artifact for a defined audience, purpose, and medium.

lessons.title: Communication and Media Choice

learning_objectives.statement: Identify sender, receiver, purpose, context, and feedback in communication.
learning_objectives.label: B1.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Compare media by persistence, reach, interaction, detail, and accessibility.
learning_objectives.label: B1.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Select a medium for a stated audience and justify the choice.
learning_objectives.label: B1.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Choose and justify communication media for contrasting scenarios.

lessons.title: Digital Representation of Media

learning_objectives.statement: Explain that text, images, and sound require defined digital representations.
learning_objectives.label: B2.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Predict how resolution, sampling, or encoding choices affect fidelity and size.
learning_objectives.label: B2.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Choose a representation appropriate to a communication purpose.
learning_objectives.label: B2.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Reason about representation trade-offs without depending on one software tool.

lessons.title: Audience, Purpose, and Accessibility

learning_objectives.statement: Describe an audience without relying on a single assumed user.
learning_objectives.label: B3.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Identify barriers caused by language, vision, hearing, movement, device, or context.
learning_objectives.label: B3.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Turn an audience and purpose statement into accessible design requirements.
learning_objectives.label: B3.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Review a fictional artifact for barriers and propose testable improvements.

lessons.title: Information Structure and Visual Hierarchy

learning_objectives.statement: Group and order information according to audience tasks.
learning_objectives.label: B4.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Use headings, labels, spacing, and emphasis to create a perceivable hierarchy.
learning_objectives.label: B4.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why color alone cannot carry meaning.
learning_objectives.label: B4.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Restructure content and justify each hierarchy decision against requirements.

lessons.title: Data Visualization and Misleading Presentation

learning_objectives.statement: Match a basic chart form to a communication purpose and data relationship.
learning_objectives.label: B5.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Identify truncated scales, missing context, visual distortion, and inaccessible encoding.
learning_objectives.label: B5.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Revise a visualization to communicate its evidence and limits more clearly.
learning_objectives.label: B5.O3

prerequisites: B4: Information Structure and Visual Hierarchy.
lessons.assessment_intent: Critique and redesign a synthetic-data chart without overstating conclusions.

lessons.title: Prototyping and Usability Evaluation

learning_objectives.statement: Create a low-cost prototype from communication requirements.
learning_objectives.label: B6.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Plan a task-based evaluation with observable criteria.
learning_objectives.label: B6.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Separate participant evidence from the designer's assumption and prioritize revisions.
learning_objectives.label: B6.O3

prerequisites: B4: Information Structure and Visual Hierarchy.
lessons.assessment_intent: Produce an evaluation plan and improve a prototype from recorded observations.

lessons.title: Information Design Project

learning_objectives.statement: Design an original information artifact for a stated audience and purpose.
learning_objectives.label: B7.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate supplied test evidence and inspect the revised artifact against communication and accessibility requirements.
learning_objectives.label: B7.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Use evidence to explain revisions and remaining limitations.
learning_objectives.label: B7.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Performance task with requirements, artifact, evaluation evidence, and revision rationale.

units.title: Computers, Algorithms, and Programming

source_locator.normative: High School Course of Study, Information I content (3), PDF page 192
source_locator.commentary: Information commentary, Information I content (3), PDF pages 38-42

units.unit_outcome: Explain computation, trace and create programs, compare algorithms, and use models and simulations to solve problems.

lessons.title: Computer Systems and Information Processing

learning_objectives.statement: Explain the roles of input, output, memory, processing, software, and external devices.
learning_objectives.label: C1.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Trace a simple instruction and data flow through a computer system.
learning_objectives.label: C1.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Relate digital representation and finite resources to a computation limit.
learning_objectives.label: C1.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Trace system components and explain a representation or resource limitation.

lessons.title: Variables and Assignment

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why a named stored value is useful.
learning_objectives.label: C2.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Assign and update a value in a short Python program.
learning_objectives.label: C2.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Trace stored values and printed output after ordered assignments.
learning_objectives.label: C2.O3

prerequisites: No prior programming is assumed; required arithmetic expressions and ordered execution are introduced and checked inside this lesson.
lessons.assessment_intent: Write one assignment and trace an update without introducing comparison syntax.

lessons.title: Comparisons and Conditionals

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate a Boolean comparison separately from assignment.
learning_objectives.label: C3.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Trace which branch runs for given input values.
learning_objectives.label: C3.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Write and test a simple conditional for a stated rule.
learning_objectives.label: C3.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Predict branch behavior, then implement and test a two-branch decision.

lessons.title: Repetition

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why a repeated procedure can be represented as a loop.
learning_objectives.label: C4.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Trace a fixed-count loop and a condition-controlled loop.
learning_objectives.label: C4.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Write a loop with a clear stopping condition and test its boundary.
learning_objectives.label: C4.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Trace iterations and repair a loop with an incorrect stopping condition.

lessons.title: Collections and Strings

learning_objectives.statement: Represent related values as a sequence instead of separate variables.
learning_objectives.label: C5.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Access and iterate over collection elements while respecting index boundaries.
learning_objectives.label: C5.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Choose an appropriate simple representation for text or tabular values.
learning_objectives.label: C5.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Trace collection access, transform a sequence, and justify a representation choice for text or tabular values.

lessons.title: Functions and Decomposition

learning_objectives.statement: Explain a function as a named reusable operation with inputs and an output or effect.
learning_objectives.label: C6.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Define and call a short Python function.
learning_objectives.label: C6.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Decompose a problem into functions with clear responsibilities.
learning_objectives.label: C6.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Trace arguments, parameters, return values, and call results, then refactor repeated print-job logic into functions with explicit responsibilities and no hidden state.

lessons.title: Algorithms and Efficiency

learning_objectives.statement: Express an algorithm using structured natural language or code.
learning_objectives.label: C7.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Compare algorithms for correctness, clarity, and work as input grows.
learning_objectives.label: C7.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Use tests and traces to improve an algorithm.
learning_objectives.label: C7.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Express, trace, test, and improve FIFO print scheduling while preserving the supplied request order.

lessons.title: Modeling and Simulation

learning_objectives.statement: Identify variables, assumptions, relationships, and boundaries in a simple model.
learning_objectives.label: C8.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Run a manual and a Python-based simulation for the same model.
learning_objectives.label: C8.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate how assumptions or parameter changes affect results and limitations.
learning_objectives.label: C8.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Manually trace and execute the same print-queue simulation, compare all outputs, and evaluate assumptions, sensitivity, and limits.

lessons.title: Tested Programming Project

learning_objectives.statement: Design an algorithm for a bounded problem and implement it in Python.
learning_objectives.label: C9.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Test normal, boundary, invalid, and empty cases appropriate to the design.
learning_objectives.label: C9.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate the program against requirements and explain improvements and limits.
learning_objectives.label: C9.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: FIFO-core performance task using supplied starter signatures, an executable program, tests, requirement evaluation, and reflection; shortest-job-first is an optional extension.

units.title: Networks, Information Systems, and Data

source_locator.normative: High School Course of Study, Information I content (4), PDF pages 192-193
source_locator.commentary: Information commentary, Information I content (4), PDF pages 42-48

units.unit_outcome: Explain networked services and security, then collect, manage, analyze, and communicate data with stated limitations.

lessons.title: Networks and Protocols

learning_objectives.statement: Identify endpoints, links, intermediary devices, and protocols in a small network.
learning_objectives.label: D1.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why shared communication rules are necessary.
learning_objectives.label: D1.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Design a small network from stated communication and reliability needs.
learning_objectives.label: D1.O3

prerequisites: C1: Computer Systems and Information Processing.
lessons.assessment_intent: Interpret and improve a small network diagram without vendor-specific configuration.

lessons.title: Internet, Web, and Information Services

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish the Internet, the Web, and an information service.
learning_objectives.label: D2.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Trace a simplified name-resolution, request-response, data-processing, and service-delivery path.
learning_objectives.label: D2.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate how an information system's components and operation support or limit effective service use.
learning_objectives.label: D2.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Trace a simplified service flow, diagnose a failure location, and evaluate service use against a stated need.

lessons.title: Authentication, Encryption, and Security

learning_objectives.statement: Relate confidentiality, integrity, and availability to concrete risks.
learning_objectives.label: D3.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Distinguish authentication, authorization, encryption, and backup.
learning_objectives.label: D3.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Select layered controls for a scenario and explain residual risk.
learning_objectives.label: D3.O3

prerequisites: A3: Rights, Responsibility, Privacy, and Risk.
D2: Internet, Web, and Information Services.
lessons.assessment_intent: Build a threat-control table using current primary technical sources where claims require them.

lessons.title: Data Lifecycle, Measurement, Bias, and Ethics

learning_objectives.statement: Describe how a question becomes measured, collected, stored, used, and deleted data.
learning_objectives.label: D4.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Identify sampling, measurement, missing-data, and representation bias.
learning_objectives.label: D4.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Plan proportionate collection that accounts for purpose, consent, privacy, and minimization.
learning_objectives.label: D4.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Critique a synthetic collection plan and revise it to reduce avoidable bias and risk.

lessons.title: Tabular Data and Cleaning

learning_objectives.statement: Identify observations, variables, values, types, and missing data in a table.
learning_objectives.label: D5.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Apply reproducible cleaning rules without silently changing meaning.
learning_objectives.label: D5.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Document transformations and unresolved quality limitations.
learning_objectives.label: D5.O3

prerequisites: D4: Data Lifecycle, Measurement, Bias, and Ethics.
lessons.assessment_intent: Clean a small synthetic dataset and justify each transformation.

lessons.title: Descriptive Analysis

learning_objectives.statement: Choose summaries appropriate to data type and analytical question.
learning_objectives.label: D6.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Calculate and interpret counts, proportions, center, and spread for small datasets.
learning_objectives.label: D6.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Explain how distributions or outliers can make one summary misleading.
learning_objectives.label: D6.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Select and interpret summaries while stating what they do not establish.

lessons.title: Visualization and Interpretation

learning_objectives.statement: Select a table or chart for comparison, relationship, change, or classification.
learning_objectives.label: D7.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Interpret patterns while separating association from causation.
learning_objectives.label: D7.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Evaluate and improve an analysis using labels, context, uncertainty, and accessible encodings.
learning_objectives.label: D7.O3

prerequisites: B5: Data Visualization and Misleading Presentation.
lessons.assessment_intent: Create and critique an accessible visualization from synthetic or licensed data.

lessons.title: Databases and Queries

learning_objectives.statement: Explain why structured data storage supports consistent retrieval and update.
learning_objectives.label: D8.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Identify records, fields, keys, tables, and relationships in a small database.
learning_objectives.label: D8.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Construct and evaluate simple filter, projection, sort, and aggregate queries.
learning_objectives.label: D8.O3

prerequisites: D2: Internet, Web, and Information Services.
lessons.assessment_intent: Design and query a small offline database without personal or sensitive data.

lessons.title: Data Investigation Project

learning_objectives.statement: Turn a bounded question into an ethical and reproducible data investigation.
learning_objectives.label: D9.O1

learning_objectives.statement: Collect or select, clean, analyze, visualize, and document data.
learning_objectives.label: D9.O2

learning_objectives.statement: Communicate a conclusion with uncertainty, limitations, and improvement proposals.
learning_objectives.label: D9.O3

lessons.assessment_intent: Performance task with question, provenance, transformations, analysis, communication, and reflection criteria.

global_prerequisites: Everyday experience using digital information and communication tools.
Basic arithmetic including whole numbers, decimals, and simple proportions; required ideas are refreshed before use.
Basic chart reading from earlier schooling; no advanced statistics or prior programming is assumed.
numbering_convention: A1-D9 are planning and learner navigation labels. Stable lesson IDs and existing file paths remain unchanged; historical Lesson 01 refers to C2 only.
